    Suzannah Wistreich

    I am a coterm Master's student and incoming PhD in Computer Science at Stanford University where I research multisensory learning, robotics, and computer vision advised by Professor Jiajun Wu.

    I graduated from Stanford University with a Bachelor's in Computer Science (2025). In my undergraduate, I was very fortunate to be advised by Professor Jiajun Wu and Professor Hyo Gweon, working on robotics and human cognition.

    ⚙️ This summer, I will be interning at Boston Dynamics, working on the Atlas AI team!

    News

    • [April 2026] Invited to talk about DexSkin at the Leonardo Art Science Evening Rendezvous (LASER) lecture series. (Details)
    • [February 2026] Accepted to CS PhD program at Stanford University.
    • [September 2025] Selected as a Siebel Scholar, Class of 2026, Stanford School of Engineering.
    • [August 2025] DexSkin: High-Coverage Conformable Robotic Skin for Learning Contact-Rich Manipulation, is accepted to CoRL 2025!
    • [June 2025] X-Capture: An Open-Source Portable Device for Multi-Sensory Learning, is accepted to ICCV 2025!
    • [June 2025] Completed BSCS degree at Stanford University.

    Publications

    DexSkin: High-Coverage Conformable Robotic Skin for Learning Contact-Rich Manipulation

    Suzannah Wistreich*, Baiyu Shi*, Stephen Tian*, Samuel Clarke, Michael Nath, Chengyi Xu, Zhenan Bao, Jiajun Wu

    Conference on Robot Learning (CoRL), 2025 (Oral)
